text.title: jira-workflow-management Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for jira-workflow-management as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Adding global transitions to "Done" from any state.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Global-to-Done transitions let issues skip QA and review stages, producing false "Done" counts and audit trail gaps.

failure-mode.description: Creating automation rules that fire on issue creation without a label filter.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Broad creation triggers fire on every issue in the project, including bulk imports, dramatically inflating execution counts and frequently producing incorrect results on migrated issues.

failure-mode.description: Agents writing field names from training knowledge rather than the live Jira field schema.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Jira custom field IDs differ per instance. Cache /rest/api/3/field and provide the schema in the system prompt; reject any output containing field names not in the schema.

failure-mode.description: Bulk transitions without rate-limit handling on Jira Cloud.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Jira Cloud enforces approximately 50 req/s per site. Unthrottled bulk transitions hit 429s immediately; exponential backoff with Retry-After header is required.

failure-mode.description: Applying workflows designed for Jira Cloud to a Data Center or Server instance.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cloud and DC/Server have different automation engines, API surfaces, and permission models. Code working on one frequently fails on the other.
